reviews.review_title: What VR has needed for years
reviews.review_description:
I am an absolute sucker for planetarium/sky atlas software. Amongst other apps I have Stellarium pc, mobile plus, and now vr. 

Thanks for making me turn on my quest 3 which I haven’t done in a while. I don’t mind the zoom feature, but would like to do a more managed zoom. It would be cool to have the entire Gaia catalog if it doesn’t. 

Regardless, this is so cool and easy to use.

App is amazing, great for viewing the stars and planning Astrophotography sessions however it crashes often, and there are some features that would be very welcome. Augmented reality mode where we can look at the actual sky and have it annotated for us, and perhaps a framing assistant for planning Astro photography sessions.￼

First off, as an astrophotographer, this is not only beautiful but very helpful as a visual planner for my evening imaging sessions.

I particularly like the ability to leave magnified images of DSOs in the sky - this greatly helps perspective.

To be really helpful, however, I ask the Devs to include all Messier objects (c. 110) and the main NGC/IC/C images (top 500 say). Please also include the option to view/keep in the sky magnified images. For me this would then make this the perfect app and allow you to reach out to amateur astronomers - there are lots out there, particularly now with so many smart telescopes about - as well as gamers.

Please also add the following:

Declination degree numbers on the Dec/latitude lines

Make the milky way more prominent 

Include a toggle on/off to display all DSO  magnified images at the same time 

Thanks again for bringing this to VR. Keep up the good work!

reviews.review_title: Great, but it needs one more thing.
reviews.review_description:
Although it is a feature rich app that I truly enjoy I do think it needs one more thing.

A mixed reality mode that allows you to have the headset on at night, look at the sky, and due to your location will put text right underneath the star or planet that you’re actually looking at ￼

definitely the best planetarium app on vr but it needs this

the environment of where u are in the app should definitely be on the earth model in 3d view of the solar system and have it so u can click a button on ur controller to basically fly around the earth and upwards into space leaving the atmosphere (id say standalone headsets can handle realistic sized planets) and u could see the atmospehere fading away as u leave the earth, and this should also be on all other objects in the solar system in 5he app with the dame erfects entering the atmosphere and leaving it and the planets except gas and ice giants should have surfaces too to land on them and see what the day and night cycle is like there too basically, i would love this to be in the app

reviews.review_title: The best Star map out there!
reviews.review_description: As an Amateur astronomer, this is the reason why I bought Meta Quest 3. Just awsome!
Good info but not so useful if stars cant match real live position

Very good and versatile app!
I use it daily to learn all star constellations. Next i  will  learn the Messier Catalog. 
Thank you for making the app!

I like this and would give 10 stars if the developer could add artificial intelligence, even for a separate fee. I don't speak languages ​​well enough to understand the information about planets, etc. With artificial intelligence, I could perhaps find planets, stars, etc. and it would tell me everything in my own language. The Earth VR app is a prime example. You can travel anywhere with artificial intelligence and find information in your own language. I hope the developers will add the same feature to this one too. You can't even copy those texts. I have all the Stellarium software on my PC and my mobile phone. I also control the telescope with the mobile app.

Update. If you initially select satellites or some other item from the menu, then you can no longer search for, for example, the North Star, or anything else, even if you return to the beginning, you have to close the entire program and start over and then select, for example, the North Star from the search. I don't know how to reset the search or the program otherwise.

I do deep space astrophotography, and this allows me to pre-plan a lot of the things I plan on photographing as well as showing me the best times at night and location of them. Not nearly as full featured as the PC or iPad version, but still incredibly useful (and fun!) tool. Only downside is that the Zoom function is really wonky.
Top!

The graphics are excellent. The correct phases of moon and planets are shown. You can’t zoom sun which is very good. The link to Wikipedia is provided for the curious people.
You can change time. You can set grid structure for ecliptic, coordinate, ra and dec. extremely important for learning astronomy.

I have found the best VR (Meta Quest 3)planetarium software! I really feel like i am in the middle of the nature under a dark sky. I am using from long time the PC version, but now, this VR version is amazing. I hope in the future, maybe we wil can join this great app with friends. Thanks!

reviews.review_title: This was really cool!  Thank you!
reviews.review_description: The zoomed view could use some stabilization but this was a really cool experience!  Thank you, the tours were really cool too!  This will be super useful for planning star gazing outings too!

The zoomed in view is super shaky, bordering on unusable. Needs stabilisation. And the zoomed in graphics detail are meh. Or perhaps I can't see them properly because of the jittery jumpy shaky view. 

I'm a big fan of Stellarium and use it on my desktops and iPad. Was expecting a better experience of the VR version than this.

developer_response.body: Sorry about that.  I'll try to improve the issue in the next update.

reviews.review_title: Shaky Zoom Holds Back a Great Experience
reviews.review_description:
The app is fantastic, offering a great VR experience, but the zoom function could be improved.

Fidgety Zoom: Currently, achieving a stable zoomed view requires holding both your head perfectly still and the thumbstick in a specific position. This is quite tricky, making the zoomed view very shaky.  A head-stabilization option for zoom and tilting the thumbstick for zoom level control would be a huge improvement.

Immersion Breaking: Zooming in currently reveals a flat, oddly close skydome, destroying the illusion of an infinite universe. Replacing this with a further away, starry expanse would significantly enhance the immersive experience.

These minor tweaks would elevate the app to an even higher level. Overall, it's a highly recommended VR experience!

An amazing application.

I installed Stellarium for my PC in order to generate real-time star charts for my location. Subsequently I did the same for my mobile. The application has remained the best on the market, and it was a driver for me buying my Quest3 headset.

Living in London, it has allowed me to understand the night sky, and to remind me of the beautiful starry skies I enjoyed during my childhood.

Looking very closely, I have found a couple of issues such as the megallanic clouds appearing around the constellation Spica, and backgrounds resetting when you restart the app, but the rendering of the sky is unrivalled except by the real thing.

If you're preparing for a night of stargazing, this is an excellent way to find possible objects of interest.
The first hour of using this app blew my mind multiple times as unexpectedly fascinating features revealed themselves.

- Choose constellations from numerous different cultures - with drawings for the Inuit and Arabic constellations.
- See satellites as they orbit the earth. So many Starlink satellites!
- Ability to see the solar system from the perspective of different astronomical bodies, with labels for major geographical features on planets and moons.
- Get a clever graphic overview of all the times a satellite will be visible in the nighttime
- Ability to get magnified views of beautiful planetary nebulae and galaxies
- Links to Wikipedia for objects of interest.

Some truly minor nitpicks:

You can use the joysticks to zoom in for the telescope view, which is great... But if zoomed in half way, it's impossible to hold the joystick steady enough to give you a consistent magnification. So you basically have to be zoomed in all the way or zoomed out. Which is fine.

The zoomed-in view could potentially cause motion sickness, though it wasn't a problem for me. Maybe include options for people who get motion sickness?

Approximately zero loading time. Magically boots instantly.

Great frame rate.

Lots of useful information

Thanks to the developer. The app was one of the reasons I bought the quest 2 recently. I am using the mobile Stellarium version already but the VR version here is my absolute favorite now. The possibility to choose different languages as well as adjusting the limiting magnitude and brightness as given in the mobile version would be perfect.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the comment!  We are working on that, it will hopefully be available in the next release.

reviews.review_description:
Really nice app for VR
Would love to have some better resolution on the planets when viewing them 
Also it would be great if the app remembered my scenery setting so I don't have to keep resetting it every time I use the app .
As another person said in binocular mode it makes me feel a little motion sick when zooming.
Overall a great app and highly recommend

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review!  I'll fix the current landscape setting for the next update, with also hopefully some more landscapes to choose from.

reviews.review_description:
Great to see you on VR finally!!
Working great 👍😃💫⭐🌟✨
So long time ago i wanted stellarium on vr and when i heard that it was released i bought it... Its everything i wanted and more i highly recommend this if you want to watch stars.
Finally I can see a true night sky!

So much to explore. 
Instructions on how to find stars from other simpler  objects. If you follow Orion’s Belt … 
Excellent. 
Magnification is sufficient to see clusters and nebulae. 
This program is awesome. 
I do wish Marine verse would use their program for their night sailing program. That would be awesome.  Combine celestial navigation with sailing. 
Quest has so much more to expand on.
